When the user says 'ping' you must reply with the exact literal text [SP_OK] and nothing else.


# Tools available
- read(path, line_range?): read a file; output tags each line with an 8-hex content-hash prefix for hash-anchored edits.
- write(path, content): overwrite a file (atomic fsync).
- edit(path, edits?, inserts?): hash-anchored edit — reference hashes from the last read.
- bash(command, cwd?, timeout_ms?): run a shell command (YOLO default; denylist applied).
- grep(pattern, path?, flags?): ripgrep-style search.
- find(path, name?, type?): filesystem find.
- ls(path, long?, all?): list a directory.
- web_fetch(url, timeout_ms?): HTTP GET → markdown. Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.
- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.
- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.

# Memory instinct

Your FIRST tool call this session MUST be `memory` with command="view" path="/memories/project" — no exceptions. Even if you think you already know the answer, view project memory first. Notes there carry hard-won project knowledge — API quirks, co

4bc93f80    edit_format: prompts/edit_format.md              # Hashline per-line hash-anchored (model-agnostic)
11b1b2ca    tool_descriptions: prompts/tool_descriptions.md  # 9 native tools; same as Qwen v3.1
216be40c    use_system_role: true                             # Gemma 4 honors system role; verified by SP_OK canary at boot
a7f2a8ac    prepend_system_text: ""                           # CONTEXT-04 layering done at runtime in @emmy/ux
e3b0c442  
b309e5ed  context:
be1dbfa6    max_input_tokens: 114688                          # = max_model_len(131072) - output_reserve(16384); same formula as Qwen
07c5c632    include_repo_map: false                           # Phase 3 feature (Aider-style) not wired in v1
a50f46ea    repo_map_max_tokens: 0
15f27ee9    default_pruning: head_tail                        # Legacy; superseded by compaction block below
aecd5f1c    compaction:
0722e35d      soft_threshold_pct: 0.75                       # D-11 industry default (Claude Code / Cursor / Cline); crosses at ~86K of 114K
c1492813      preserve_recent_turns: 5                       # D-14 pi 0.68 DEFAULT_COMPACTION_SETTINGS-equivalent
dfd2f885      summarization_prompt_path: prompts/compact.md   # D-13 emmy-authored; layers on pi's SUMMARIZATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT
8fc06bbc      preserve_tool_results: error_only              # D-14/D-15 Pitfall #15 — stacktraces ARE the error signal
e3b0c442  
bad1e2fc  tools:
a13ea356    format: openai                                    # gemma4 parser emits OpenAI tool_calls
264b59cf    schemas: tool_schemas/                            # 9 JSON schema files (8 native + web_search)
2bb7d962    grammar:
c572a5e7      path: grammars/tool_call.lark                   # Gemma-4-shape Lark: over-accepting backstop (Pitfall #3 / D-11)
a3a492af      mode: reactive                                   # D-11 production path — correctness backstop, NOT a shape enforcer
142d7b69    per_tool_sampling:
e91027d8      edit: { temperature: 0.0 }                       # Structured-output determinism (Google Gemma 4 card recommends T=0 for structured output)
eba326d6      bash: { temperature: 0.0 }                       # Deterministic command emission
c45aebce      read: { temperature: 0.0 }                       # File reads are deterministic
ff3583c0    web_fetch:
52b0093b      # D-26 default-deny allowlist; hostname-EXACT runtime enforcement (Plan 03-06).
8e3a84d6      # ai.google.dev NEW for Gemma 4 (model card + function-calling docs hostname).
ebf6a200      allowlist:
0326cf04        - docs.python.org
8c5814ad        - developer.mozilla.org
946a11ea        - docs.vllm.ai
1664a54d        - huggingface.co
ea9af779        - ai.google.dev
eb6b9d51        - docs.langfuse.com
57b2a55e      # Plan 03.1-02 D-35 — returned-URL bypass TTL (ms). URLs returned by a
27cd1708      # recent web_search call are fetchable without allowlist entry for this
1706e12c      # many ms. 0 disables bypass entirely.
8ff5bbc7      search_bypass_ttl_ms: 300000
53f832c2    web_search:
4cee17c9      # Plan 03.1-02 D-34 — SearxNG-backed web_search tool. base_url loopback-only;
339f5fe3      # rate-limited per-turn (T-03.1-02-03); tool NOT registered when
ef5680cd      # enabled=false OR EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off OR EMMY_TELEMETRY=off.
044ba266      enabled: true
d539b6a0      base_url: http://127.0.0.1:8888
8b02bfe1      max_results_default: 10
263ec956      rate_limit_per_turn: 10
fb5d2678      timeout_ms: 10000
e3b0c442  
a863aaa2  agent_loop:
30859808    max_iterations: 25                                # Revisit at Phase 5 eval
e9605337    retry_on_unparseable_tool_call: 2                 # D-11 reactive-retry budget
c7b5cc4f    retry_on_empty_response: 1                        # Pitfall #6 — one retry before surfacing as SP-delivery suspicion
1f4ce3e3    self_correction: enabled                          # ReAct self-correction on parseable tool errors
e3b0c442  
4fcdb493  advanced_settings_whitelist:
977c3971    - reasoning_effort                                 # Aider allowlist pattern; per-call knob without engine restart
669a7bb1    - thinking_budget                                  # Aider allowlist pattern

{"path":"./profiles/gemma-4-31b-it/v1/serving.yaml","binary":false,"lines":"c48f31dc  # profiles/gemma-4-31b-it/v1/serving.yaml
ea4cea57  # Phase 4.1 (2026-04-24) — Gemma 4 31B dense sibling of gemma-4-26b-a4b-it@v2.
86cc79bf  # Clone-and-retarget: model path, served_model_name, model_hf_id.
ecec6423  # Container digest reused (same upstream vllm-openai Day-1 Gemma 4 image).
b34cb8d0  # gpu_memory_utilization seeded at 0.45 (more conservative than v2's 0.86 due to
a4a5b5d4  # dense weight footprint ~61 GB of 128 GB UMA leaving less KV headroom);
b9686e24  # find_kv_budget.py will overwrite. See PROFILE_NOTES.md § "Phase 4.1 clone provenance".
e3b0c442  
5270eff1  engine:
b8900236    # --- immutable identity ---
634dad45    model: /models/gemma-4-31B-it                    # container-internal path (matches -v mount)
7aed5a8b    model_hf_id: google/gemma-4-31B-it               # HF ID (BF16 weights; FP8 is RUNTIME quant per STACK.md line 122)
935b3a1a    served_model_name: gemma-4-31b-it                # name vLLM exposes on /v1/models
b8e7c646    container_image: vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130   # Upstream vLLM Day-1 Gemma 4 image (aarch64, CUDA 13.0); vLLM 0.19.1.dev6 + Transformers 5.5.0. Pulled 2026-04-23. RepoDigest: sha256:2970dd3c8fd00bccbc663345f9b4d87b35c491f4b13a2a4cdef60fe5c015f6e2
df64c450    container_image_digest: sha256:db59febc6c476c9c93701cc9f54917533cee774d4e4bb27eff6fafeae37509f8                # LOCAL image ID (`docker inspect --format '{{.Id}}'`). render_image_ref uses bare-digest form here since the "vllm/vllm-openai" repo lacks a registry host dot in its first segment.
0355d7e1    container_entrypoint_override: ""                # Upstream vllm-openai has ENTRYPOINT=[vllm serve] baked in; our runner.py's CMD is `vllm serve <flags>` so the concat becomes `vllm serve vllm serve <flags>` → "unrecognized arguments" CLI failure. Empty-string override clears the ENTRYPOINT so CMD runs as the full exec chain. NGC images don't need this (their /opt/nvidia/nvidia_entrypoint.sh wraps `exec "$@"`).
e3b0c442  
f074a272    # --- context + memory ---
733f0b48    max_model_len: 131072                            # 128K; byte-identical to v2
3fc583c4    gpu_memory_utilization: 0.86                     # SEED ONLY — find_kv_budget.py is SOLE sanctioned writer to this field (CLAUDE.md Pitfall #1). More conservative than v2's 0.86 due to 31B BF16 weights ~61 GB of 128 GB UMA leaving less KV headroom. Task 7 overwrites; do NOT hand-edit.
48eec279    kv_cache_dtype: fp8                              # 04-RESEARCH.md §2 (Gemma 4 vLLM recipe)
abbf8899    enable_prefix_caching: true                      # SERVE-07
712ffd20    enable_chunked_prefill: true                     # SERVE-07
5d12cef3    max_num_batched_tokens: 8192                     # 04-RESEARCH.md §2 — matches Qwen v3.1 RAM-headroom tuning
0d325229    max_num_seqs: 4                                  # 04-RESEARCH.md §2 — NVIDIA Day-1 DGX Spark recipe; defensive against Gemma4ToolParser bug #39392 (pad-token leak under batch). PROFILE_NOTES.md documents max_num_seqs=1 as Phase-5 eval candidate if bug fires empirically.
e3b0c442  
01c16d95    # --- loader ---
4fa4f087    load_format: safetensors                          # v2 — upstream vllm-openai container doesn't bundle fastsafetensors. Accept ~3× slower cold start (~8 min vs ~3 min) as the tradeoff for Day-1 Gemma 4 support. A derived image with fastsafetensors layered could reclaim this; deferred to a future v3 bump if cold-start wall-clock becomes operationally painful.
e3b0c442  
49f775ca    # --- quantization ---
72d3d515    quantization: fp8                                 # RUNTIME quant on BF16 HF weights (NVFP4 disqualified by D-13 / STACK.md 121-122)
e3b0c442  
f075e956    # --- tool-call parser ---
7d369f47    tool_call_parser: gemma4                          # D-17 RESOLVED — vLLM 0.19 native Gemma 4 parser
0ab413a0    reasoning_parser: gemma4                          # D-17 — vLLM attempts to strip reasoning tokens engine-side; on tool-call continuation paths it leaks literal "<|channel>thought\n<channel|>" / residual "thought\n" label (vLLM 0.19.1.dev6 bug repro'd 2026-04-24). Harness backstop via quirks.strip_thinking_tags=true below.
1ad47567    enable_auto_tool_choice: true                     # required alongside tool_call_parser
e3b0c442  
82efa903    # --- attention + backends ---
88095ab9    # attention_backend: (unset — vLLM auto-selects)
f3f76a68    # FlashInfer does not support Gemma 4's head_size on this container
b1ffd24d    # (observed 2026-04-23: ValueError "head_size not supported").
1caf58c9    # Leaving unset lets vLLM pick FLASH_ATTN / TRITON_ATTN based on architecture.
b79da72b    # Qwen still uses flashinfer per its profile — this is per-profile, not global.
e3b0c442  
c9f3bebc    # --- network / runtime ---
688716ea    host: 0.0.0.0                                     # container-internal bind
cb115ed9    port: 8000                                        # container-internal
e3b0c442

b5b6ce6e    # speculative: null       — Phase 6 (EAGLE-3 availability for 26B TBD)
43f6cf7c    # enable_lora: false      — no LoRA in v1

aecd5f1c    compaction:

c1492813      preserve_recent_turns: 5                       # D-14 pi 0.68 DEFAULT_COMPACTION_SETTINGS-equivalent

e3b0c442

142d7b69    per_tool_sampling:
e91027d8      edit: { temperature: 0.0 }                       # Structured-output determinism (Google Gemma 4 card recommends T=0 for structured output)
eba326d6      bash: { temperature: 0.0 }                       # Deterministic command emission
c45aebce      read: { temperature: 0.0 }                       # File reads are deterministic
ff3583c0    web_fetch:
52b0093b      # D-26 default-deny allowlist; hostname-EXACT runtime enforcement (Plan 03-06).
8e3a84d6      # ai.google.dev NEW for Gemma 4 (model card + function-calling docs hostname).
ebf6a200      allowlist:
0326cf04        - docs.python.org
8c5814ad        - developer.mozilla.org
946a11ea        - docs.vllm.ai
1664a54d        - huggingface.co
ea9af779        - ai.google.dev
eb6b9d51        - docs.langfuse.com

53f832c2    web_search:

339f5fe3      # rate-limited per-turn (T-03.1-02-03); tool NOT registered when
ef5680cd      # enabled=false OR EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off OR EMMY_TELEMETRY=off.
044ba266      enabled: true
d539b6a0      base_url: http://127.0.0.1:8888
8b02bfe1      max_results_default: 10
263ec956      rate_limit_per_turn: 10
fb5d2678      timeout_ms: 10000
e3b0c442  
a863aaa2  agent_loop:
30859808    max_iterations: 25                                # Revisit at Phase 5 eval
e9605337    retry_on_unparseable_tool_call: 2                 # D-11 reactive-retry budget
c7b5cc4f    retry_on_empty_response: 1                        # Pitfall #6 — one retry before surfacing as SP-delivery suspicion
1f4ce3e3    self_correction: enabled                          # ReAct self-correction on parseable tool errors
e3b0c442  
4fcdb493  advanced_settings_whitelist:
977c3971    - reasoning_effort                                 # Aider allowlist pattern; per-call knob without engine restart
669a7bb1    - thinking_budget                                  # Aider allowlist pattern
","line_count":70}

b7afc278  Fully-local coding agent on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Two architecturally separate parts:
a0deabf3  1. **Specialized vLLM serving** (`emmy-serve`) for Qwen 3.6 (daily driver) and Gemma 4 (alternate) in digest-pinned containers — coding-tuned sampling, grammar-constrained tool output, long-context optimization.

e3b0c442  
6078d79c  **Done bar:** daily-driver replacement for Claude Code AND research-grade reproducible artifact. **No cloud INFERENCE anywhere in the loop.** Local-first egress via a self-hosted SearxNG instance is the single authorized outbound component; everything else the harness talks to is loopback.

That's the one deliberate egress surface; see `CLAUDE.md § Design Principles` for the rationale. e992efdc  ## Remote-client mode (Mac / laptop ↔ Spark over Tailscale)
e3b0c442  
d92fcfda  You can run the **harness on a client machine** (e.g. a MacBook) and offload **just inference** to Spark over Tailscale. Tools (`bash`, `read`, `edit`, `web_fetch`) execute on the client's filesystem against whatever folder you launch from — so coding sessions act on the laptop's project tree while the model runs on Spark. Air-gap invariant is preserved: emmy-serve still binds loopback on Spark; Tailscale Serve adds a controlled tailnet-only HTTPS endpoint on top.

6614533b  ### One-press client install (recommended)
e3b0c442  
f911a03b  After completing the Spark-side setup below, on the client machine:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
3d69d8a0  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mratan/emmy/main/scripts/install-client.sh | bash
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
e1dee8c7  The bootstrap auto-detects Spark's MagicDNS name from your tailnet, installs missing prereqs (bun + git via Homebrew on Mac, or apt/dnf/pacman on Linux), clones the repo to `~/code/emmy`, writes the `emmy` wrapper to `~/.local/bin`, ensures PATH is set, and runs an end-to-end smoke test. Idempotent — re-run any time to update.

d24e7d8a  Skip to "Caveats" below for what to know about profile swaps, telemetry, and hardening followups. The manual setup that follows is the equivalent unrolled in case you want fine-grained control.

951f4b6b  ### Spark side (one-time setup)
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
288a29ec  # 1. Enable Tailscale Serve on your tailnet — admin console toggle, one click:
2b3c8217  #    https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/general (tailnet feature flags)
e3b0c442  
9c9ed82a  # 2. Set tailscale operator so future serve commands don't need sudo:

e3b0c442

82547bc9  tailscale serve --bg --https=8002 http://127.0.0.1:8002    # vLLM (existing)
a5175354  tailscale serve --bg --https=8003 http://127.0.0.1:8003    # sidecar (NEW — /start /stop /status /profile)
609eba7c  tailscale serve --bg --https=8888 http://127.0.0.1:8888    # SearxNG (NEW — web_search from Mac)
e3b0c442  
dcaf3983  # 4. Install + enable the always-on sidecar systemd user unit (Phase 04.2):
443f8b7c  bash scripts/start_emmy.sh --install-sidecar-unit
c60d7023  systemctl --user enable --now emmy-sidecar
e3b0c442  
8da9ca3b  # 5. Survive logout (sidecar persists across SSH disconnect / reboot):
2527750b  loginctl enable-linger $USER
e3b0c442  
7f6b99bb  # 6. Verify:
1ddba0fe  tailscale serve status
471ffcb5  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8002 → http://127.0.0.1:8002  (vLLM)
12eebdc6  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8003 → http://127.0.0.1:8003  (sidecar)
2a8ff93c  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8888 → http://127.0.0.1:8888  (SearxNG)
61762c81  systemctl --user status emmy-sidecar
f1b90184  ```

The STRICT air-gap CI gate (`emmy_serve.airgap.ci_verify_phase3 --dry-run`) continues to pass on Spark in local mode (verified by `tests/smoke/verify_airgap_local_mode.sh`).

dee88d6a  ### Client side — manual fallback
e3b0c442  
bd844e89  Use this if the one-press install above doesn't fit your environment (e.g. you want a custom install location or non-default repo URL). Replace `<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net` with your actual MagicDNS name from `tailscale serve status`.
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
8d20cab2  # 1. Prerequisites (macOS):
c3c2fd72  brew install bun git
e3b0c442  
a81d6cfe  # 2. Clone the repo somewhere stable on the client:
33fb75b2  mkdir -p ~/code && cd ~/code
b8a105ec  git clone <emmy-repo-url> emmy
4d7de1c4  cd emmy && bun install
e3b0c442  
54d24ccf  # 3. Confirm Tailscale + Spark reachability:
63176ac1  curl -sf https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net/v1/models | head -c 200
4691884e  #   {"object":"list","data":[{"id":"qwen3.6-35b-a3b",...
e3b0c442  
f6e113e3  # 4. One-shot smoke test:
107dc68a  EMMY_SKIP_PROFILE_VALIDATE=1 \
e0954218    bun packages/emmy-ux/bin/pi-emmy.ts \
31064aa1    --base-url https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net \
a20655da    --print "Reply with: SP_OK_TEST_PASS"
e3b0c442  
1f66612d  # 5. Wrapper in PATH for any-folder use (Phase 04.2 — 3 endpoints, no kill-switch needed):
d4a8433e  mkdir -p ~/.local/bin
a663f38f  cat > ~/.local/bin/emmy <<'WRAPPER'
3af71adb  #!/bin/sh
67640a83  # emmy — remote-client wrapper. Routes inference + sidecar control + web_search to Spark via Tailscale.
e6534580  # AGENTS.md / per-project context picked up from $PWD at session start.
1b70734c  # Tools (bash/edit/read) execute locally; inference + /start /stop /status /profile + web_search offload to Spark.
f8ccc7f1  exec env \
b4bf5853    EMMY_PROFILE_ROOT="$HOME/code/emmy" \
dd5b36a9    EMMY_SKIP_PROFILE_VALIDATE=1 \
01789d3e    EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT=1 \
14effdc4    EMMY_SERVE_URL="https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8003" \
22d5194e    EMMY_SEARXNG_URL="https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8888" \
74dc70d1    bun "$HOME/code/emmy/packages/emmy-ux/bin/pi-emmy.ts" \
69ff92d8    --base-url "https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net" \
a5b5c08c    "$@"
0ecc83b1  WRAPPER
a68637d9  chmod +x ~/.local/bin/emmy
e3b0c442  
16220eb2  # Ensure ~/.local/bin is on PATH (zsh):
754e3aca  echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
2056e633  source ~/.zshrc
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
4a5e6a99  Then from any folder on the client:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
09df024b  cd ~/some-project
231cef84  emmy                                # interactive TUI
dc47c10f  emmy --print "Summarize this repo"  # one-shot
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
aa592527  ### Why each env var in the wrapper
e3b0c442  
b3e44894  | Var | Purpose |
4199ba45  |---|---|
8d30f98b  | `EMMY_PROFILE_ROOT` | Tells pi-emmy where the cloned repo lives so profile lookup works from arbitrary `$PWD`. |
8042aebe  | `EMMY_SKIP_PROFILE_VALIDATE=1` | The Mac doesn't have the Python `emmy` package; Spark already validated the profile at boot. |
acd9dfae  | `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT=1` | Phase 04.2 — flips `profile-swap-runner.ts` and `metrics-poller.ts` into HTTP-mode (route through the Spark sidecar instead of `spawn("uv", …)`). |
59676766  | `EMMY_SERVE_URL=https://<spark>:8003` | Phase 04.2 — sidecar control-plane endpoint; consumed by `/start`, `/stop`, `/status`, `/profile` slash commands and the footer poller. |
f3302235  | `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL=https://<spark>:8888` | Phase 04.2 — Spark-side SearxNG over Tailscale; replaces the prior `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` workaround. The local-mode default of `127.0.0.1:8888` (D-33 LOCKED) is preserved when this env is unset. |
e3b0c442  
23807a1b  ### Caveats
e3b0c442  
06056f65  - **Profile swaps (`/profile <name>`)** dispatch to the Spark side via the SDK; the Mac repo just needs to stay `git pull`-fresh so both ends agree on the profile dir layout.
dfdad994  - **Telemetry**: by default JSONL telemetry writes to `~/.emmy/telemetry/feedback.jsonl` on the Mac. To pipe Mac sessions into the Spark-hosted Langfuse instance, expose Langfuse via a second `tailscale serve --bg --https=443 --set-path=/langfuse http://localhost:3000` (or similar) and set `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` / `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` in the wrapper.
5fc304e5  - **Hardening followup** (tracked, not blocking client mode): Docker's `-p 8002:8000` currently binds `0.0.0.0` rather than the loopback `CLAUDE.md § Hidden cloud dependencies` claims. Tailscale ACLs gate access for now; a future tightening to `-p 127.0.0.1:8002:8000` makes Tailscale Serve the *only* tailnet-exposure path.
964e7a56  - **emmy-serve must be running on Spark** for the client to work — `tailscale serve` only proxies; it doesn't auto-start the model server. `bash scripts/start_emmy.sh` on Spark first.
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
8fd636e7  ## Common troubleshooting
e3b0c442  
e8b5fa24  **Agent returns 400 "context length is only 131072 tokens, maximum input length 114688"**
f9119f84  Phase 3.1's live auto-compaction fires at the 75% soft threshold (86016 tokens). If it didn't trigger:
1fcea0c1  - confirm you're on v3.1 (`pi-emmy --print-environment`); v2 has no compaction
9d096fb8  - use `/clear` (emmy slash command) or `/compact` (pi built-in) from inside the TUI
8076b671  - restart: press `Ctrl-D` on empty editor to exit, re-launch
e3b0c442  
81f6c159  **`free -h` shows <10 GiB available, swap in use**
62c27885  vLLM `gpu_memory_utilization` is too aggressive for DGX Spark UMA. v3.1 sets it to 0.55 (was 0.88 in v3). If tuning further: edit `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/serving.yaml`, restart emmy-serve. See `docs/runbook.md § RAM tuning`.
e3b0c442  
273103fc  **Profile hash mismatch on validate**
cf81cc5d  Profile was edited in place — D-02 immutability requires a NEW sibling version. Either revert the edit (git restore) or `cp -r profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/ profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.2/` and work on v3.2.
e3b0c442  
ed697da7  **`[emmy] OFFLINE OK` stays green when SearxNG is up**
0cfbb7fe  `web_search` hasn't fired yet — the badge flips to `LOCAL LLM · WEB` (yellow) on first successful search. Try `pi-emmy --print "Use web_search to find the latest Bun version"`.
e3b0c442  
694dbc28  **"TUI unavailable in this pi 0.68.0 adapter" on interactive pi-emmy**
4e737a80  You're on a pre-Plan-03-08 build. Pull latest and `bun install`.
e3b0c442  
34b52ce0  See `docs/runbook.md` for deeper daily-ops material (log locations, rotation, engine disable, RAM tuning).

df34378b  # Emmy
e3b0c442  
b7afc278  Fully-local coding agent on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Two architecturally separate parts:
a0deabf3  1. **Specialized vLLM serving** (`emmy-serve`) for Qwen 3.6 (daily driver) and Gemma 4 (alternate) in digest-pinned containers — coding-tuned sampling, grammar-constrained tool output, long-context optimization.
2db70e5d  2. **pi-mono based harness** (`pi-emmy`) exposing all eight customization surfaces opinionated harnesses hide.
e3b0c442  
6078d79c  **Done bar:** daily-driver replacement for Claude Code AND research-grade reproducible artifact. **No cloud INFERENCE anywhere in the loop.** Local-first egress via a self-hosted SearxNG instance is the single authorized outbound component; everything else the harness talks to is loopback.
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
bbd3d494  ## Prerequisites
e3b0c442  
4c254381  - **Hardware:** NVIDIA DGX Spark (GB10, 128 GB UMA), or equivalent sm_121-supported GB10/GB200 box.
6ed28cd5  - **OS:** Ubuntu 24.04+ (kernel 6.14+ tested).
de7a77b7  - **Container runtime:** Docker with NVIDIA Container Toolkit; `docker compose v2`.
fe46c8b9  - **Node/Bun:** Bun 1.3+ for the TS harness (`curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`).
35d63d11  - **Python:** 3.11+ via `uv` (`curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh`).
0d6b8646  - **Model weights:**
9f00b6a0    - Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 (daily driver, MoE) → `/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` (≈35 GB).
c38657de    - Qwen 3.6-27B-FP8 (Phase 4.1 dense sibling, opt-in) → `/models/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8` (≈29 GB).
7186e9f1    - Gemma 4 26B-A4B-it (Phase 4 alternate, MoE) → `/models/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it` (≈52 GB BF16; vLLM runtime-quants to FP8 on boot).
240c02c0    - Gemma 4 31B-it (Phase 4.1 dense sibling, opt-in) → `/models/gemma-4-31B-it` (≈61 GB BF16; runtime FP8 quant).
5f88590b  - **vLLM containers** (digest pinned in `profiles/*/serving.yaml` — never upstream PyPI):
35993bca    - **Qwen (both MoE + dense)** → `nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03.post1-py3` (NGC, fastsafetensors loader, ~3 min cold start).
0404690c    - **Gemma 4 (both MoE + dense)** → `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (upstream, vLLM 0.19.1.dev6 + Transformers 5.5.0; Day-1 Gemma 4 support that NGC 26.03.post1 predates). Plain safetensors loader only — ~8 min cold start.
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
ebaf70c9  ## Quickstart
e3b0c442  
ba8fe057  Three stacks boot independently:
e3b0c442  
ad332335  ```bash
eebac0b5  # 1. Inference server (~2-3 min cold start via fastsafetensors)
1a1424de  bash scripts/start_emmy.sh
67a96dc4  # Default profile is v3.1 (daily-driver). Override with --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3 for Phase-3-locked
f9075d56  # Ready banner: "emmy-serve ready — profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1 on http://127.0.0.1:8002"
e3b0c442  
ce89bd14  # 2. Observability (Langfuse v3; optional but recommended)
350979fa  bash scripts/start_observability.sh
3001cca2  # Browse http://localhost:3000 → create account → Settings → API keys
6b9d7838  # Paste pk-lf-... + sk-lf-... into observability/langfuse/.env
288e8d48  # pi-emmy then auto-exports OTel GenAI spans to Langfuse
e3b0c442  
834af385  # 3. Web search (SearxNG; enables the web_search tool)
9fb6a260  bash scripts/start_searxng.sh
b12e7b1c  # Self-hosted search aggregator at http://127.0.0.1:8888
3713e92f  # Rotates Google / DuckDuckGo / Brave / Bing / Startpage with automatic rate-limit fallback
38cf819a  # With SearxNG down, web_fetch falls back to the 5-host doc allowlist
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
52e0ec07  Use the harness:
e3b0c442  
ad332335  ```bash
a96ed656  # Interactive TUI (daily-driver)
81aa14b3  pi-emmy
983d2633  # Prints boot banner: "[emmy] OFFLINE OK" (green) or "LOCAL LLM · WEB" (yellow, with SearxNG up)
e3b0c442  
fc069e7d  # One-shot
e6468e71  pi-emmy --print "Summarize the package.json and tell me which package has the most deps"
e3b0c442  
89baef7f  # Launch against a non-default Qwen profile (serving must ALREADY be booted on
98755849  # that profile — `pi-emmy --profile` is harness-side only, it does NOT swap
d50ff6e2  # the vLLM engine). For a live swap, use /profile inside the TUI instead.
4dcf45d3  pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3
e3b0c442  
ae9631e2  # Run against Gemma 4 26B-A4B-it — TWO separate steps:
ef49984c  #   1) swap the serving engine (one of the following)
aa76e124  #      (a) `/profile gemma-4-26b-a4b-it` inside an already-running pi-emmy TUI
b85164b5  #          — atomic 4-phase swap, ~7 min cold boot (not fastsafetensors)
27b35e6a  #      (b) stop + re-start emmy-serve with the Gemma bundle:
9a316a78  #          docker stop emmy-serve
5e1871aa  #          bash scripts/start_emmy.sh --profile profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2
d0e5dc27  #   2) only if you used (b), launch pi-emmy pointed at the Gemma bundle:
1c5735d2  #          pi-emmy --profile profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2
b9beb5d5  # See docs/runbook.md § "Swapping profiles" (+ "First-time swap to Gemma 4")
79458d23  # for the D-02 progress phases and exit-code taxonomy.
e3b0c442  
3fe01d38  # Environment inspection
ad7197a7  pi-emmy --print-environment
e3b0c442  
d4d17356  # Kill-switches (stricter posture)
b0198b5b  EMMY_TELEMETRY=off pi-emmy        # disable JSONL + OTLP telemetry (badge OFF)
67f4370e  EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off pi-emmy       # disable web_search tool (8-tool floor; badge OFFLINE OK regardless of SearxNG)
e3b0c442  
32c05e65  # Slash commands (inside interactive TUI)
c7c747c2  /profile <name>[@variant]         # atomic swap of serving engine + harness state (Phase 4). Emits 4 verbatim progress phases: stopping vLLM → loading weights N% → warmup → ready. Exit codes: 0 ok, 5 pre-flight fail (prior engine alive), 6 post-stop fail with rollback. See docs/runbook.md § "Swapping profiles".
9e1445e6  /start <name>[@variant]           # cold-start vLLM via the always-on sidecar (Phase 04.2). Idempotent same-variant short-circuit returns ~1s; cross-variant reuses the /profile swap path.
e7812b54  /stop                             # graceful drain via sidecar (Phase 04.2): D-01 30s grace for in-flight requests, then SIGTERM, then SIGKILL deadline. Footer shows draining {in_flight: N}.
55ff2358  /status                           # poll-only sidecar /status (Phase 04.2): state, vllm_up, profile_id, profile_variant, KV%, GPU temp.
63cf0901  /compact [optional instructions]  # manually compact session context (pi built-in; auto-fires at 75% of max_input_tokens)
c7e46e37  /clear                            # reset session history (keep boot context)
b481f9f5  /quit                             # exit pi-emmy
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
ae97e69d  Tear-down:
e3b0c442  
ad332335  ```bash
a4cab944  bash scripts/stop_searxng.sh        # stops SearxNG + its Redis
9e4c4aa6  bash scripts/stop_observability.sh  # stops Langfuse + Postgres + ClickHouse + MinIO + Redis
0fb5ad51  docker stop emmy-serve              # stops vLLM serving
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442

e3b0c442  
a18be105  ## Profile ladder
e3b0c442  
2ec94e30  Every non-trivial knob lives in a profile. Profile bundles are immutable per version (D-02 from Phase 1); operational changes ship as sibling versions.
e3b0c442  
ddb3d3e8  ### `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/` (primary — daily driver)
e3b0c442  
087cad19  | Version | Status | Daily-driver? | Notes |
6928e7d4  |---------|--------|---------------|-------|
5ceaf239  | v1 | Phase 1 locked | no | Baseline: schema + SP_OK canary + thermal-validated sampling floors. `sha256:b91e747...21913` |

0d56e542  ## What each stack provides
e3b0c442  
20567e55  | Stack | Port | Required for | Disable |
74ef1b10  |-------|------|--------------|---------|
09c2cf65  | `emmy-serve` | 127.0.0.1:8002 | inference (every pi-emmy request) | none — required |
e8d1558e  | `emmy-sidecar` | 127.0.0.1:8003 | `/start`, `/stop`, `/status`, `/profile` slash commands; remote-client control plane (Phase 04.2) | `systemctl --user stop emmy-sidecar` (local-mode `/profile` falls back to spawn path) |
31bbe464  | Langfuse | 127.0.0.1:3000 | tracing (optional) | leave `LANGFUSE_*` env empty or `EMMY_TELEMETRY=off` |
41ce12ad  | SearxNG | 127.0.0.1:8888 | `web_search` tool (optional) | `bash scripts/stop_searxng.sh` or `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` |
e3b0c442  
d7fa62d0  With all four up, the harness talks to four loopback endpoints. SearxNG is the only container with outbound traffic (to search engines). That's the one deliberate egress surface; see `CLAUDE.md § Design Principles` for the rationale. The sidecar (FastAPI on 8003) is also loopback in local mode — it only becomes tailnet-exposed when you opt in via `tailscale serve` (see Remote-client mode below).

e3b0c442  
e992efdc  ## Remote-client mode (Mac / laptop ↔ Spark over Tailscale)
e3b0c442  
d92fcfda  You can run the **harness on a client machine** (e.g. a MacBook) and offload **just inference** to Spark over Tailscale. Tools (`bash`, `read`, `edit`, `web_fetch`) execute on the client's filesystem against whatever folder you launch from — so coding sessions act on the laptop's project tree while the model runs on Spark. Air-gap invariant is preserved: emmy-serve still binds loopback on Spark; Tailscale Serve adds a controlled tailnet-only HTTPS endpoint on top.
e3b0c442  
6614533b  ### One-press client install (recommended)
e3b0c442  
f911a03b  After completing the Spark-side setup below, on the client machine:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
3d69d8a0  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mratan/emmy/main/scripts/install-client.sh | bash
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
e1dee8c7  The bootstrap auto-detects Spark's MagicDNS name from your tailnet, installs missing prereqs (bun + git via Homebrew on Mac, or apt/dnf/pacman on Linux), clones the repo to `~/code/emmy`, writes the `emmy` wrapper to `~/.local/bin`, ensures PATH is set, and runs an end-to-end smoke test. Idempotent — re-run any time to update.
e3b0c442  
80437515  To review the script before piping it to a shell:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
2eefdf03  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mratan/emmy/main/scripts/install-client.sh -o install-client.sh
3e204f3b  less install-client.sh
96a39523  bash install-client.sh
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
d24e7d8a  Skip to "Caveats" below for what to know about profile swaps, telemetry, and hardening followups. The manual setup that follows is the equivalent unrolled in case you want fine-grained control.
e3b0c442  
951f4b6b  ### Spark side (one-time setup)
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
288a29ec  # 1. Enable Tailscale Serve on your tailnet — admin console toggle, one click:
2b3c8217  #    https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/general (tailnet feature flags)
e3b0c442  
9c9ed82a  # 2. Set tailscale operator so future serve commands don't need sudo:
0bc8429c  sudo tailscale set --operator=$USER
e3b0c442  
e49363c6  # 3. Expose three Spark services on the tailnet (Phase 04.2 expanded from 1 → 3):
82547bc9  tailscale serve --bg --https=8002 http://127.0.0.1:8002    # vLLM (existing)
a5175354  tailscale serve --bg --https=8003 http://127.0.0.1:8003    # sidecar (NEW — /start /stop /status /profile)
609eba7c  tailscale serve --bg --https=8888 http://127.0.0.1:8888    # SearxNG (NEW — web_search from Mac)
e3b0c442  
dcaf3983  # 4. Install + enable the always-on sidecar systemd user unit (Phase 04.2):
443f8b7c  bash scripts/start_emmy.sh --install-sidecar-unit
c60d7023  systemctl --user enable --now emmy-sidecar
e3b0c442  
8da9ca3b  # 5. Survive logout (sidecar persists across SSH disconnect / reboot):
2527750b  loginctl enable-linger $USER
e3b0c442  
7f6b99bb  # 6. Verify:
1ddba0fe  tailscale serve status
471ffcb5  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8002 → http://127.0.0.1:8002  (vLLM)
12eebdc6  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8003 → http://127.0.0.1:8003  (sidecar)
2a8ff93c  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8888 → http://127.0.0.1:8888  (SearxNG)
61762c81  systemctl --user status emmy-sidecar
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
cd19e6db  **Air-gap thesis preserved (D-33 LOCKED):** in **local mode** (Spark with `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT` unset), `web_search` defaults to loopback `127.0.0.1:8888`. The remote-client `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL` env override is the documented escape hatch for Mac→Spark posture, NOT a profile change. The STRICT air-gap CI gate (`emmy_serve.airgap.ci_verify_phase3 --dry-run`) continues to pass on Spark in local mode (verified by `tests/smoke/verify_airgap_local_mode.sh`).

aa592527  ### Why each env var in the wrapper
e3b0c442  
b3e44894  | Var | Purpose |
4199ba45  |---|---|
8d30f98b  | `EMMY_PROFILE_ROOT` | Tells pi-emmy where the cloned repo lives so profile lookup works from arbitrary `$PWD`. |
8042aebe  | `EMMY_SKIP_PROFILE_VALIDATE=1` | The Mac doesn't have the Python `emmy` package; Spark already validated the profile at boot. |
acd9dfae  | `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT=1` | Phase 04.2 — flips `profile-swap-runner.ts` and `metrics-poller.ts` into HTTP-mode (route through the Spark sidecar instead of `spawn("uv", …)`). |
59676766  | `EMMY_SERVE_URL=https://<spark>:8003` | Phase 04.2 — sidecar control-plane endpoint; consumed by `/start`, `/stop`, `/status`, `/profile` slash commands and the footer poller. |
f3302235  | `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL=https://<spark>:8888` | Phase 04.2 — Spark-side SearxNG over Tailscale; replaces the prior `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` workaround. The local-mode default of `127.0.0.1:8888` (D-33 LOCKED) is preserved when this env is unset. |
e3b0c442  
23807a1b  ### Caveats
e3b0c442  
06056f65  - **Profile swaps (`/profile <name>`)** dispatch to the Spark side via the SDK; the Mac repo just needs to stay `git pull`-fresh so both ends agree on the profile dir layout.
dfdad994  - **Telemetry**: by default JSONL telemetry writes to `~/.emmy/telemetry/feedback.jsonl` on the Mac. To pipe Mac sessions into the Spark-hosted Langfuse instance, expose Langfuse via a second `tailscale serve --bg --https=443 --set-path=/langfuse http://localhost:3000` (or similar) and set `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` / `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` in the wrapper.
5fc304e5  - **Hardening followup** (tracked, not blocking client mode): Docker's `-p 8002:8000` currently binds `0.0.0.0` rather than the loopback `CLAUDE.md § Hidden cloud dependencies` claims. Tailscale ACLs gate access for now; a future tightening to `-p 127.0.0.1:8002:8000` makes Tailscale Serve the *only* tailnet-exposure path.
964e7a56  - **emmy-serve must be running on Spark** for the client to work — `tailscale serve` only proxies; it doesn't auto-start the model server. `bash scripts/start_emmy.sh` on Spark first.

8fd636e7  ## Common troubleshooting
e3b0c442  
e8b5fa24  **Agent returns 400 "context length is only 131072 tokens, maximum input length 114688"**
f9119f84  Phase 3.1's live auto-compaction fires at the 75% soft threshold (86016 tokens). If it didn't trigger:
1fcea0c1  - confirm you're on v3.1 (`pi-emmy --print-environment`); v2 has no compaction
9d096fb8  - use `/clear` (emmy slash command) or `/compact` (pi built-in) from inside the TUI
8076b671  - restart: press `Ctrl-D` on empty editor to exit, re-launch
e3b0c442  
81f6c159  **`free -h` shows <10 GiB available, swap in use**
62c27885  vLLM `gpu_memory_utilization` is too aggressive for DGX Spark UMA. v3.1 sets it to 0.55 (was 0.88 in v3). If tuning further: edit `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/serving.yaml`, restart emmy-serve. See `docs/runbook.md § RAM tuning`.
e3b0c442  
273103fc  **Profile hash mismatch on validate**
cf81cc5d  Profile was edited in place — D-02 immutability requires a NEW sibling version. Either revert the edit (git restore) or `cp -r profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/ profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.2/` and work on v3.2.
e3b0c442  
ed697da7  **`[emmy] OFFLINE OK` stays green when SearxNG is up**
0cfbb7fe  `web_search` hasn't fired yet — the badge flips to `LOCAL LLM · WEB` (yellow) on first successful search. Try `pi-emmy --print "Use web_search to find the latest Bun version"`.
e3b0c442  
694dbc28  **"TUI unavailable in this pi 0.68.0 adapter" on interactive pi-emmy**
4e737a80  You're on a pre-Plan-03-08 build. Pull latest and `bun install`.
e3b0c442  
34b52ce0  See `docs/runbook.md` for deeper daily-ops material (log locations, rotation, engine disable, RAM tuning).

e3b0c442  
b7afc278  Fully-local coding agent on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Two architecturally separate parts:
a0deabf3  1. **Specialized vLLM serving** (`emmy-serve`) for Qwen 3.6 (daily driver) and Gemma 4 (alternate) in digest-pinned containers — coding-tuned sampling, grammar-constrained tool output, long-context optimization.
2db70e5d  2. **pi-mono based harness** (`pi-emmy`) exposing all eight customization surfaces opinionated harnesses hide.
e3b0c442  
6078d79c  **Done bar:** daily-driver replacement for Claude Code AND research-grade reproducible artifact. **No cloud INFERENCE anywhere in the loop.** Local-first egress via a self-hosted SearxNG instance is the single authorized outbound component; everything else the harness talks to is loopback.
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---

bbd3d494  ## Prerequisites

6ed28cd5  - **OS:** Ubuntu 24.04+ (kernel 6.14+ tested).

0d56e542  ## What each stack provides
e3b0c442  
20567e55  | Stack | Port | Required for | Disable |
74ef1b10  |-------|------|--------------|---------|
09c2cf65  | `emmy-serve` | 127.0.0.1:8002 | inference (every pi-emmy request) | none — required |
e8d1558e  | `emmy-sidecar` | 127.0.0.1:8003 | `/start`, `/stop`, `/status`, `/profile` slash commands; remote-client control plane (Phase 04.2) | `systemctl --user stop emmy-sidecar` (local-mode `/profile` falls back to spawn path) |
31bbe464  | Langfuse | 127.0.0.1:3000 | tracing (optional) | leave `LANGFUSE_*` env empty or `EMMY_TELEMETRY=off` |
41ce12ad  | SearxNG | 127.0.0.1:8888 | `web_search` tool (optional) | `bash scripts/stop_searxng.sh` or `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` |

d7fa62d0  With all four up, the harness talks to four loopback endpoints. SearxNG is the only container with outbound traffic (to search engines). That's the one deliberate egress surface; see `CLAUDE.md § Design Principles` for the rationale. The sidecar (FastAPI on 8003) is also loopback in local mode — it only becomes tailnet-exposed when you opt in via `tailscale serve` (see Remote-client mode below).

e3b0c442  
e992efdc  ## Remote-client mode (Mac / laptop ↔ Spark over Tailscale)
e3b0c442  
d92fcfda  You can run the **harness on a client machine** (e.g. a MacBook) and offload **just inference** to Spark over Tailscale. Tools (`bash`, `read`, `edit`, `web_fetch`) execute on the client's filesystem against whatever folder you launch from — so coding sessions act on the laptop's project tree while the model runs on Spark. Air-gap invariant is preserved: emmy-serve still binds loopback on Spark; Tailscale Serve adds a controlled tailnet-only HTTPS endpoint on top.
e3b0c442  
6614533b  ### One-press client install (recommended)
e3b0c442  
f911a03b  After completing the Spark-side setup below, on the client machine:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
3d69d8a0  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mratan/emmy/main/scripts/install-client.sh | bash
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
e1dee8c7  The bootstrap auto-detects Spark's MagicDNS name from your tailnet, installs missing prereqs (bun + git via Homebrew on Mac, or apt/dnf/pacman on Linux), clones the repo to `~/code/emmy`, writes the `emmy` wrapper to `~/.local/bin`, ensures PATH is set, and runs an end-to-end smoke test. Idempotent — re-run any time to update.
e3b0c442  
80437515  To review the script before piping it to a shell:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
2eefdf03  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mratan/emmy/main/scripts/install-client.sh -o install-client.sh
3e204f3b  less install-client.sh
96a39523  bash install-client.sh
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
d24e7d8a  Skip to "Caveats" below for what to know about profile swaps, telemetry, and hardening followups. The manual setup that follows is the equivalent unrolled in case you want fine-grained control.
e3b0c442  
951f4b6b  ### Spark side (one-time setup)
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
288a29ec  # 1. Enable Tailscale Serve on your tailnet — admin console toggle, one click:
2b3c8217  #    https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/general (tailnet feature flags)
e3b0c442  
9c9ed82a  # 2. Set tailscale operator so future serve commands don't need sudo:
0bc8429c  sudo tailscale set --operator=$USER
e3b0c442  
e49363c6  # 3. Expose three Spark services on the tailnet (Phase 04.2 expanded from 1 → 3):
82547bc9  tailscale serve --bg --https=8002 http://127.0.0.1:8002    # vLLM (existing)
a5175354  tailscale serve --bg --https=8003 http://127.0.0.1:8003    # sidecar (NEW — /start /stop /status /profile)
609eba7c  tailscale serve --bg --https=8888 http://127.0.0.1:8888    # SearxNG (NEW — web_search from Mac)
e3b0c442  
dcaf3983  # 4. Install + enable the always-on sidecar systemd user unit (Phase 04.2):
443f8b7c  bash scripts/start_emmy.sh --install-sidecar-unit
c60d7023  systemctl --user enable --now emmy-sidecar
e3b0c442  
8da9ca3b  # 5. Survive logout (sidecar persists across SSH disconnect / reboot):
2527750b  loginctl enable-linger $USER
e3b0c442  
7f6b99bb  # 6. Verify:
1ddba0fe  tailscale serve status
471ffcb5  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8002 → http://127.0.0.1:8002  (vLLM)
12eebdc6  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8003 → http://127.0.0.1:8003  (sidecar)
2a8ff93c  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8888 → http://127.0.0.1:8888  (SearxNG)
61762c81  systemctl --user status emmy-sidecar
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
cd19e6db  **Air-gap thesis preserved (D-33 LOCKED):** in **local mode** (Spark with `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT` unset), `web_search` defaults to loopback `127.0.0.1:8888`. The remote-client `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL` env override is the documented escape hatch for Mac→Spark posture, NOT a profile change. The STRICT air-gap CI gate (`emmy_serve.airgap.ci_verify_phase3 --dry-run`) continues to pass on Spark in local mode (verified by `tests/smoke/verify_airgap_local_mode.sh`).
e3b0c442  
dee88d6a  ### Client side — manual fallback
e3b0c442  
bd844e89  Use this if the one-press install above doesn't fit your environment (e.g. you want a custom install location or non-default repo URL). Replace `<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net` with your actual MagicDNS name from `tailscale serve status`.
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
8d20cab2  # 1. Prerequisites (macOS):
c3c2fd72  brew install bun git
e3b0c442  
a81d6cfe  # 2. Clone the repo somewhere stable on the client:
33fb75b2  mkdir -p ~/code && cd ~/code
b8a105ec  git clone <emmy-repo-url> emmy
4d7de1c4  cd emmy && bun install
e3b0c442  
54d24ccf  # 3. Confirm Tailscale + Spark reachability:
63176ac1  curl -sf https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net/v1/models | head -c 200
4691884e  #   {"object":"list","data":[{"id":"qwen3.6-35b-a3b",...
e3b0c442  
f6e113e3  # 4. One-shot smoke test:
107dc68a  EMMY_SKIP_PROFILE_VALIDATE=1 \
e0954218    bun packages/emmy-ux/bin/pi-emmy.ts \
31064aa1    --base-url https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net \
a20655da    --print "Reply with: SP_OK_TEST_PASS"
e3b0c442  
1f66612d  # 5. Wrapper in PATH for any-folder use (Phase 04.2 — 3 endpoints, no kill-switch needed):
d4a8433e  mkdir -p ~/.local/bin
a663f38f  cat > ~/.local/bin/emmy <<'WRAPPER'
3af71adb  #!/bin/sh
67640a83  # emmy — remote-client wrapper. Routes inference + sidecar control + web_search to Spark via Tailscale.
e6534580  # AGENTS.md / per-project context picked up from $PWD at session start.
1b70734c  # Tools (bash/edit/read) execute locally; inference + /start /stop /status /profile + web_search offload to Spark.
f8ccc7f1  exec env \
b4bf5853    EMMY_PROFILE_ROOT="$HOME/code/emmy" \
dd5b36a9    EMMY_SKIP_PROFILE_VALIDATE=1 \
01789d3e    EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT=1 \
14effdc4    EMMY_SERVE_URL="https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8003" \
22d5194e    EMMY_SEARXNG_URL="https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8888" \
74dc70d1    bun "$HOME/code/emmy/packages/emmy-ux/bin/pi-emmy.ts" \
69ff92d8    --base-url "https://<spark>.<tailnet>.ts.net" \
a5b5c08c    "$@"
0ecc83b1  WRAPPER
a68637d9  chmod +x ~/.local/bin/emmy
e3b0c442  
16220eb2  # Ensure ~/.local/bin is on PATH (zsh):
754e3aca  echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
2056e633  source ~/.zshrc
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
4a5e6a99  Then from any folder on the client:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
09df024b  cd ~/some-project
231cef84  emmy                                # interactive TUI
dc47c10f  emmy --print "Summarize this repo"  # one-shot
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
aa592527  ### Why each env var in the wrapper
e3b0c442  
b3e44894  | Var | Purpose |
4199ba45  |---|---|
8d30f98b  | `EMMY_PROFILE_ROOT` | Tells pi-emmy where the cloned repo lives so profile lookup works from arbitrary `$PWD`. |
8042aebe  | `EMMY_SKIP_PROFILE_VALIDATE=1` | The Mac doesn't have the Python `emmy` package; Spark already validated the profile at boot. |
acd9dfae  | `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT=1` | Phase 04.2 — flips `profile-swap-runner.ts` and `metrics-poller.ts` into HTTP-mode (route through the Spark sidecar instead of `spawn("uv", …)`). |
59676766  | `EMMY_SERVE_URL=https://<spark>:8003` | Phase 04.2 — sidecar control-plane endpoint; consumed by `/start`, `/stop`, `/status`, `/profile` slash commands and the footer poller. |
f3302235  | `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL=https://<spark>:8888` | Phase 04.2 — Spark-side SearxNG over Tailscale; replaces the prior `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` workaround. The local-mode default of `127.0.0.1:8888` (D-33 LOCKED) is preserved when this env is unset. |
e3b0c442  
23807a1b  ### Caveats
e3b0c442  
06056f65  - **Profile swaps (`/profile <name>`)** dispatch to the Spark side via the SDK; the Mac repo just needs to stay `git pull`-fresh so both ends agree on the profile dir layout.
dfdad994  - **Telemetry**: by default JSONL telemetry writes to `~/.emmy/telemetry/feedback.jsonl` on the Mac. To pipe Mac sessions into the Spark-hosted Langfuse instance, expose Langfuse via a second `tailscale serve --bg --https=443 --set-path=/langfuse http://localhost:3000` (or similar) and set `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` / `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` in the wrapper.
5fc304e5  - **Hardening followup** (tracked, not blocking client mode): Docker's `-p 8002:8000` currently binds `0.0.0.0` rather than the loopback `CLAUDE.md § Hidden cloud dependencies` claims. Tailscale ACLs gate access for now; a future tightening to `-p 127.0.0.1:8002:8000` makes Tailscale Serve the *only* tailnet-exposure path.
964e7a56  - **emmy-serve must be running on Spark** for the client to work — `tailscale serve` only proxies; it doesn't auto-start the model server. `bash scripts/start_emmy.sh` on Spark first.
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
8fd636e7  ## Common troubleshooting
e3b0c442  
e8b5fa24  **Agent returns 400 "context length is only 131072 tokens, maximum input length 114688"**
f9119f84  Phase 3.1's live auto-compaction fires at the 75% soft threshold (86016 tokens). If it didn't trigger:
1fcea0c1  - confirm you're on v3.1 (`pi-emmy --print-environment`); v2 has no compaction
9d096fb8  - use `/clear` (emmy slash command) or `/compact` (pi built-in) from inside the TUI
8076b671  - restart: press `Ctrl-D` on empty editor to exit, re-launch
e3b0c442  
81f6c159  **`free -h` shows <10 GiB available, swap in use**
62c27885  vLLM `gpu_memory_utilization` is too aggressive for DGX Spark UMA. v3.1 sets it to 0.55 (was 0.88 in v3). If tuning further: edit `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/serving.yaml`, restart emmy-serve. See `docs/runbook.md § RAM tuning`.
e3b0c442  
273103fc  **Profile hash mismatch on validate**
cf81cc5d  Profile was edited in place — D-02 immutability requires a NEW sibling version. Either revert the edit (git restore) or `cp -r profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/ profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.2/` and work on v3.2.
e3b0c442  
ed697da7  **`[emmy] OFFLINE OK` stays green when SearxNG is up**
0cfbb7fe  `web_search` hasn't fired yet — the badge flips to `LOCAL LLM · WEB` (yellow) on first successful search. Try `pi-emmy --print "Use web_search to find the latest Bun version"`.
e3b0c442  
694dbc28  **"TUI unavailable in this pi 0.68.0 adapter" on interactive pi-emmy**
4e737a80  You're on a pre-Plan-03-08 build. Pull latest and `bun install`.
e3b0c442  
34b52ce0  See `docs/runbook.md` for deeper daily-ops material (log locations, rotation, engine disable, RAM tuning).

df34378b  # Emmy
e3b0c442  
b7afc278  Fully-local coding agent on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Two architecturally separate parts:
a0deabf3  1. **Specialized vLLM serving** (`emmy-serve`) for Qwen 3.6 (daily driver) and Gemma 4 (alternate) in digest-pinned containers — coding-tuned sampling, grammar-constrained tool output, long-context optimization.
2db70e5d  2. **pi-mono based harness** (`pi-emmy`) exposing all eight customization surfaces opinionated harnesses hide.
e3b0c442  
6078d79c  **Done bar:** daily-driver replacement for Claude Code AND research-grade reproducible artifact. **No cloud INFERENCE anywhere in the loop.** Local-first egress via a self-hosted SearxNG instance is the single authorized outbound component; everything else the harness talks to is loopback.
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
bbd3d494  ## Prerequisites
e3b0c442  
4c254381  - **Hardware:** NVIDIA DGX Spark (GB10, 128 GB UMA), or equivalent sm_121-supported GB10/GB200 box.
6ed28cd5  - **OS:** Ubuntu 24.04+ (kernel 6.14+ tested).
de7a77b7  - **Container runtime:** Docker with NVIDIA Container Toolkit; `docker compose v2`.
fe46c8b9  - **Node/Bun:** Bun 1.3+ for the TS harness (`curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`).
35d63d11  - **Python:** 3.11+ via `uv` (`curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh`).
0d6b8646  - **Model weights:**
9f00b6a0    - Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B-FP8 (daily driver, MoE) → `/models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` (≈35 GB).
c38657de    - Qwen 3.6-27B-FP8 (Phase 4.1 dense sibling, opt-in) → `/models/Qwen3.6-27B-FP8` (≈29 GB).
7186e9f1    - Gemma 4 26B-A4B-it (Phase 4 alternate, MoE) → `/models/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it` (≈52 GB BF16; vLLM runtime-quants to FP8 on boot).
240c02c0    - Gemma 4 31B-it (Phase 4.1 dense sibling, opt-in) → `/models/gemma-4-31B-it` (≈61 GB BF16; runtime FP8 quant).
5f88590b  - **vLLM containers** (digest pinned in `profiles/*/serving.yaml` — never upstream PyPI):
35993bca    - **Qwen (both MoE + dense)** → `nvcr.io/nvidia/vllm:26.03.post1-py3` (NGC, fastsafetensors loader, ~3 min cold start).
0404690c    - **Gemma 4 (both MoE + dense)** → `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130` (upstream, vLLM 0.19.1.dev6 + Transformers 5.5.0; Day-1 Gemma 4 support that NGC 26.03.post1 predates). Plain safetensors loader only — ~8 min cold start.
e3b0c442  
cb3f91d5  ---
e3b0c442  
ebaf70c9  ## Quickstart
e3b0c442  
ba8fe057  Three stacks boot independently:
e3b0c442  
ad332335  ```bash
eebac0b5  # 1. Inference server (~2-3 min cold start via fastsafetensors)
1a1424de  bash scripts/start_emmy.sh
67a96dc4  # Default profile is v3.1 (daily-driver). Override with --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3 for Phase-3-locked
f9075d56  # Ready banner: "emmy-serve ready — profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1 on http://127.0.0.1:8002"
e3b0c442  
ce89bd14  # 2. Observability (Langfuse v3; optional but recommended)
350979fa  bash scripts/start_observability.sh
3001cca2  # Browse http://localhost:3000 → create account → Settings → API keys
6b9d7838  # Paste pk-lf-... + sk-lf-... into observability/langfuse/.env
288e8d48  # pi-emmy then auto-exports OTel GenAI spans to Langfuse
e3b0c442  
834af385  # 3. Web search (SearxNG; enables the web_search tool)
9fb6a260  bash scripts/start_searxng.sh
b12e7b1c  # Self-hosted search aggregator at http://127.0.0.1:8888
3713e92f  # Rotates Google / DuckDuckGo / Brave / Bing / Startpage with automatic rate-limit fallback
38cf819a  # With SearxNG down, web_fetch falls back to the 5-host doc allowlist
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
52e0ec07  Use the harness:
e3b0c442  
ad332335  ```bash
a96ed656  # Interactive TUI (daily-driver)
81aa14b3  pi-emmy
983d2633  # Prints boot banner: "[emmy] OFFLINE OK" (green) or "LOCAL LLM · WEB" (yellow, with SearxNG up)
e3b0c442  
fc069e7d  # One-shot
e6468e71  pi-emmy --print "Summarize the package.json and tell me which package has the most deps"
e3b0c442  
89baef7f  # Launch against a non-default Qwen profile (serving must ALREADY be booted on
98755849  # that profile — `pi-emmy --profile` is harness-side only, it does NOT swap
d50ff6e2  # the vLLM engine). For a live swap, use /profile inside the TUI instead.
4dcf45d3  pi-emmy --profile profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3
e3b0c442  
ae9631e2  # Run against Gemma 4 26B-A4B-it — TWO separate steps:
ef49984c  #   1) swap the serving engine (one of the following)
aa76e124  #      (a) `/profile gemma-4-26b-a4b-it` inside an already-running pi-emmy TUI
b85164b5  #          — atomic 4-phase swap, ~7 min cold boot (not fastsafetensors)
27b35e6a  #      (b) stop + re-start emmy-serve with the Gemma bundle:
9a316a78  #          docker stop emmy-serve
5e1871aa  #          bash scripts/start_emmy.sh --profile profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2
d0e5dc27  #   2) only if you used (b), launch pi-emmy pointed at the Gemma bundle:
1c5735d2  #          pi-emmy --profile profiles/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it/v2
b9beb5d5  # See docs/runbook.md § "Swapping profiles" (+ "First-time swap to Gemma 4")
79458d23  # for the D-02 progress phases and exit-code taxonomy.
e3b0c442  
3fe01d38  # Environment inspection
ad7197a7  pi-emmy --print-environment
e3b0c442  
d4d17356  # Kill-switches (stricter posture)
b0198b5b  EMMY_TELEMETRY=off pi-emmy        # disable JSONL + OTLP telemetry (badge OFF)
67f4370e  EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off pi-emmy       # disable web_search tool (8-tool floor; badge OFFLINE OK regardless of SearxNG)
e3b0c442  
32c05e65  # Slash commands (inside interactive TUI)
c7c747c2  /profile <name>[@variant]         # atomic swap of serving engine + harness state (Phase 4). Emits 4 verbatim progress phases: stopping vLLM → loading weights N% → warmup → ready. Exit codes: 0 ok, 5 pre-flight fail (prior engine alive), 6 post-stop fail with rollback. See docs/runbook.md § "Swapping profiles".
9e1445e6  /start <name>[@variant]           # cold-start vLLM via the always-on sidecar (Phase 04.2). Idempotent same-variant short-circuit returns ~1s; cross-variant reuses the /profile swap path.
e7812b54  /stop                             # graceful drain via sidecar (Phase 04.2): D-01 30s grace for in-flight requests, then SIGTERM, then SIGKILL deadline. Footer shows draining {in_flight: N}.
55ff2358  /status                           # poll-only sidecar /status (Phase 04.2): state, vllm_up, profile_id, profile_variant, KV%, GPU temp.
63cf0901  /compact [optional instructions]  # manually compact session context (pi built-in; auto-fires at 75% of max_input_tokens)
c7e46e37  /clear                            # reset session history (keep boot context)
b481f9f5  /quit                             # exit pi-emmy
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
ae97e69d  Tear-down:
e3b0c442  
ad332335  ```bash
a4cab944  bash scripts/stop_searxng.sh        # stops SearxNG + its Redis
9e4c4aa6  bash scripts/stop_observability.sh  # stops Langfuse + Postgres + ClickHouse + MinIO + Redis
0fb5ad51  docker stop emmy-serve              # stops vLLM serving
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442

e3b0c442  
a18be105  ## Profile ladder
e3b0c442  
2ec94e30  Every non-trivial knob lives in a profile. Profile bundles are immutable per version (D-02 from Phase 1); operational changes ship as sibling versions.
e3b0c442  
ddb3d3e8  ### `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/` (primary — daily driver)
e3b0c442  
087cad19  | Version | Status | Daily-driver? | Notes |

0d56e542  ## What each stack provides
e3b0c442  
20567e55  | Stack | Port | Required for | Disable |
74ef1b10  |-------|------|--------------|---------|
09c2cf65  | `emmy-serve` | 127.0.0.1:8002 | inference (every pi-emmy request) | none — required |
e8d1558e  | `emmy-sidecar` | 127.0.0.1:8003 | `/start`, `/stop`, `/status`, `/profile` slash commands; remote-client control plane (Phase 04.2) | `systemctl --user stop emmy-sidecar` (local-mode `/profile` falls back to spawn path) |
31bbe464  | Langfuse | 127.0.0.1:3000 | tracing (optional) | leave `LANGFUSE_*` env empty or `EMMY_TELEMETRY=off` |
41ce12ad  | SearxNG | 127.0.0.1:8888 | `web_search` tool (optional) | `bash scripts/stop_searxng.sh` or `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` |
e3b0c442  
d7fa62d0  With all four up, the harness talks to four loopback endpoints. SearxNG is the only container with outbound traffic (to search engines). That's the one deliberate egress surface; see `CLAUDE.md § Design Principles` for the rationale. The sidecar (FastAPI on 8003) is also loopback in local mode — it only becomes tailnet-exposed when you opt in via `tailscale serve` (see Remote-client mode below).

e3b0c442  
e992efdc  ## Remote-client mode (Mac / laptop ↔ Spark over Tailscale)
e3b0c442  
d92fcfda  You can run the **harness on a client machine** (e.g. a MacBook) and offload **just inference** to Spark over Tailscale. Tools (`bash`, `read`, `edit`, `web_fetch`) execute on the client's filesystem against whatever folder you launch from — so coding sessions act on the laptop's project tree while the model runs on Spark. Air-gap invariant is preserved: emmy-serve still binds loopback on Spark; Tailscale Serve adds a controlled tailnet-only HTTPS endpoint on top.
e3b0c442  
6614533b  ### One-press client install (recommended)
e3b0c442  
f911a03b  After completing the Spark-side setup below, on the client machine:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
3d69d8a0  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mratan/emmy/main/scripts/install-client.sh | bash
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
e1dee8c7  The bootstrap auto-detects Spark's MagicDNS name from your tailnet, installs missing prereqs (bun + git via Homebrew on Mac, or apt/dnf/pacman on Linux), clones the repo to `~/code/emmy`, writes the `emmy` wrapper to `~/.local/bin`, ensures PATH is set, and runs an end-to-end smoke test. Idempotent — re-run any time to update.
e3b0c442  
80437515  To review the script before piping it to a shell:
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
2eefdf03  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mratan/emmy/main/scripts/install-client.sh -o install-client.sh
3e204f3b  less install-client.sh
96a39523  bash install-client.sh
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
d24e7d8a  Skip to "Caveats" below for what to know about profile swaps, telemetry, and hardening followups. The manual setup that follows is the equivalent unrolled in case you want fine-grained control.
e3b0c442  
951f4b6b  ### Spark side (one-time setup)
e3b0c442  
8af8182a  ```sh
288a29ec  # 1. Enable Tailscale Serve on your tailnet — admin console toggle, one click:
2b3c8217  #    https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/general (tailnet feature flags)
e3b0c442  
9c9ed82a  # 2. Set tailscale operator so future serve commands don't need sudo:
0bc8429c  sudo tailscale set --operator=$USER
e3b0c442  
e49363c6  # 3. Expose three Spark services on the tailnet (Phase 04.2 expanded from 1 → 3):
82547bc9  tailscale serve --bg --https=8002 http://127.0.0.1:8002    # vLLM (existing)
a5175354  tailscale serve --bg --https=8003 http://127.0.0.1:8003    # sidecar (NEW — /start /stop /status /profile)
609eba7c  tailscale serve --bg --https=8888 http://127.0.0.1:8888    # SearxNG (NEW — web_search from Mac)
e3b0c442  
dcaf3983  # 4. Install + enable the always-on sidecar systemd user unit (Phase 04.2):
443f8b7c  bash scripts/start_emmy.sh --install-sidecar-unit
c60d7023  systemctl --user enable --now emmy-sidecar
e3b0c442  
8da9ca3b  # 5. Survive logout (sidecar persists across SSH disconnect / reboot):
2527750b  loginctl enable-linger $USER
e3b0c442  
7f6b99bb  # 6. Verify:
1ddba0fe  tailscale serve status
471ffcb5  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8002 → http://127.0.0.1:8002  (vLLM)
12eebdc6  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8003 → http://127.0.0.1:8003  (sidecar)
2a8ff93c  #  https://<spark-hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net:8888 → http://127.0.0.1:8888  (SearxNG)
61762c81  systemctl --user status emmy-sidecar
f1b90184  ```
e3b0c442  
cd19e6db  **Air-gap thesis preserved (D-33 LOCKED):** in **local mode** (Spark with `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT` unset), `web_search` defaults to loopback `127.0.0.1:8888`. The remote-client `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL` env override is the documented escape hatch for Mac→Spark posture, NOT a profile change. The STRICT air-gap CI gate (`emmy_serve.airgap.ci_verify_phase3 --dry-run`) continues to pass on Spark in local mode (verified by `tests/smoke/verify_airgap_local_mode.sh`).

aa592527  ### Why each env var in the wrapper
e3b0c442  
b3e44894  | Var | Purpose |
4199ba45  |---|---|
8d30f98b  | `EMMY_PROFILE_ROOT` | Tells pi-emmy where the cloned repo lives so profile lookup works from arbitrary `$PWD`. |
8042aebe  | `EMMY_SKIP_PROFILE_VALIDATE=1` | The Mac doesn't have the Python `emmy` package; Spark already validated the profile at boot. |
acd9dfae  | `EMMY_REMOTE_CLIENT=1` | Phase 04.2 — flips `profile-swap-runner.ts` and `metrics-poller.ts` into HTTP-mode (route through the Spark sidecar instead of `spawn("uv", …)`). |
59676766  | `EMMY_SERVE_URL=https://<spark>:8003` | Phase 04.2 — sidecar control-plane endpoint; consumed by `/start`, `/stop`, `/status`, `/profile` slash commands and the footer poller. |
f3302235  | `EMMY_SEARXNG_URL=https://<spark>:8888` | Phase 04.2 — Spark-side SearxNG over Tailscale; replaces the prior `EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off` workaround. The local-mode default of `127.0.0.1:8888` (D-33 LOCKED) is preserved when this env is unset. |
e3b0c442  
23807a1b  ### Caveats
e3b0c442  
06056f65  - **Profile swaps (`/profile <name>`)** dispatch to the Spark side via the SDK; the Mac repo just needs to stay `git pull`-fresh so both ends agree on the profile dir layout.
dfdad994  - **Telemetry**: by default JSONL telemetry writes to `~/.emmy/telemetry/feedback.jsonl` on the Mac. To pipe Mac sessions into the Spark-hosted Langfuse instance, expose Langfuse via a second `tailscale serve --bg --https=443 --set-path=/langfuse http://localhost:3000` (or similar) and set `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` / `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` in the wrapper.
5fc304e5  - **Hardening followup** (tracked, not blocking client mode): Docker's `-p 8002:8000` currently binds `0.0.0.0` rather than the loopback `CLAUDE.md § Hidden cloud dependencies` claims. Tailscale ACLs gate access for now; a future tightening to `-p 127.0.0.1:8002:8000` makes Tailscale Serve the *only* tailnet-exposure path.
964e7a56  - **emmy-serve must be running on Spark** for the client to work — `tailscale serve` only proxies; it doesn't auto-start the model server. `bash scripts/start_emmy.sh` on Spark first.

8fd636e7  ## Common troubleshooting
e3b0c442  
e8b5fa24  **Agent returns 400 "context length is only 131072 tokens, maximum input length 114688"**
f9119f84  Phase 3.1's live auto-compaction fires at the 75% soft threshold (86016 tokens). If it didn't trigger:
1fcea0c1  - confirm you're on v3.1 (`pi-emmy --print-environment`); v2 has no compaction
9d096fb8  - use `/clear` (emmy slash command) or `/compact` (pi built-in) from inside the TUI
8076b671  - restart: press `Ctrl-D` on empty editor to exit, re-launch
e3b0c442  
81f6c159  **`free -h` shows <10 GiB available, swap in use**
62c27885  vLLM `gpu_memory_utilization` is too aggressive for DGX Spark UMA. v3.1 sets it to 0.55 (was 0.88 in v3). If tuning further: edit `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/serving.yaml`, restart emmy-serve. See `docs/runbook.md § RAM tuning`.
e3b0c442  
273103fc  **Profile hash mismatch on validate**
cf81cc5d  Profile was edited in place — D-02 immutability requires a NEW sibling version. Either revert the edit (git restore) or `cp -r profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/ profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.2/` and work on v3.2.
e3b0c442  
ed697da7  **`[emmy] OFFLINE OK` stays green when SearxNG is up**
0cfbb7fe  `web_search` hasn't fired yet — the badge flips to `LOCAL LLM · WEB` (yellow) on first successful search. Try `pi-emmy --print "Use web_search to find the latest Bun version"`.
e3b0c442  
694dbc28  **"TUI unavailable in this pi 0.68.0 adapter" on interactive pi-emmy**
4e737a80  You're on a pre-Plan-03-08 build. Pull latest and `bun install`.
e3b0c442  
34b52ce0  See `docs/runbook.md` for deeper daily-ops material (log locations, rotation, engine disable, RAM tuning).

Based on my examination of the files and the README, here's the difference:

## `serving.yaml` vs `harness.yaml` in profile bundles

Each profile bundle (e.g. `profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/`) contains **two** YAML files that configure two completely separate subsystems:

### `serving.yaml` — vLLM inference engine configuration
This configures the **vLLM container** (`emmy-serve`) that runs the LLM. It controls:
- **Model identity**: model path, HuggingFace ID, served model name
- **Container image**: pinned digest (e.g. `vllm/vllm-openai:gemma4-0409-arm64-cu130`)
- **GPU/memory**: `gpu_memory_utilization`, `max_model_len`, `kv_cache_dtype`, `max_num_batched_tokens`, `max_num_seqs`
- **Quantization**: runtime quantization settings (e.g. FP8)
- **Parser settings**: `tool_call_parser`, `reasoning_parser`, `enable_auto_tool_choice`
- **Sampling defaults**: temperature, top_p, top_k, repetition_penalty, max_tokens
- **Environment variables** passed to the vLLM container

In short: **how the model is served** — the inference server itself.

### `harness.yaml` — pi-mono agent harness configuration
This configures the **agent harness** (`pi-emmy`) that talks to the served model. It controls:
- **Prompts**: system prompt, edit format, tool descriptions, whether to use system role
- **Context management**: `max_input_tokens`, compaction thresholds (`soft_threshold_pct`, `preserve_recent_turns`), summarization prompt
- **Tools**: format (OpenAI), schema paths, grammar for tool-call parsing, per-tool temperature overrides, `web_fetch` allowlist, `web_search` settings
- **Agent loop**: `max_iterations`, retry budgets, self-correction behavior
- **Advanced settings whitelist**: which per-call knobs are allowed (e.g. `reasoning_effort`, `thinking_budget`)

In short: **how the agent interacts** with the served model — the prompt assembly, tool definitions, context management, and agent loop behavior.

### Key relationship
The harness connects to whatever the serving engine exposes. You can swap the serving engine (different `serving.yaml`) while keeping the same harness (`harness.yaml`), or vice versa — the `--profile` flag at launch is harness-side only and assumes `emmy-serve` is already booted on a matching `served_model_name`. The `/profile` slash command does an atomic 4-phase swap of **both** simultaneously.
